headline: What ExxonMobil (XOM)'s Texas Move and Governance Overhaul Means For Shareholders
summary: In early July 2026, Exxon Mobil Corporation completed its redomiciliation merger to Texas, overhauled its charter and by-laws, and closed several long-running ESOP-related shelf registrations while a new, smaller board and refreshed executive team took office. These governance and structural changes coincided with a sharp oil price spike linked to renewed U.S.-Iran tensions, amplifying attention on how ExxonMobil’s tighter share structure and leaner leadership could influence its response to...

headline: Occidental Petroleum Jumps 4% While ExxonMobil, Chevron Lag: Evercore Upgrade and Oil Spike Fuel OXY’s Lead
summary: Shares of Occidental Petroleum (NYSE:OXY) are up 4% at midday Wednesday, trading at $53.90. The move puts Occidental well ahead of integrated peers ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM) and Chevron (NYSE:CVX). ExxonMobil stock is off 1% at $140.51, essentially flat. Meanwhile, Chevron shares are higher by 1% to $176.07, a modest gain that trails Occidental by a wide ...
